mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering rapid equity sell-offs over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal loses predictive power when UNRATE stays below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer prices forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations over the next quarter through higher real discount rates.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in quarterly return regressions, or positive returns dominate when both series rise together.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening corporate output and capacity utilization, which drives positive equity returns over the next month when inflation remains contained.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction or becomes insignificant when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% year-over-year, or 20-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and consumer spending power, generating positive short-term equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or positive payroll surprises fail to produce positive 5-day SPX returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding real GDP confirm synchronized demand growth across residential and broader economic sectors, supporting positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal loses predictive accuracy when HOUST declines while GDPC1 rises, or quarterly forward returns show no significant positive correlation (p > 0.10) with the interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers immediate market repricing of future monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4%, or signal IC drops below 0.02 during high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth signals resilient consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive equity returns over the next week during stable monetary conditions.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive returns when FEDFUNDS is rising rapidly, or 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of high-sales months.
